scenario.title: Celebrating a Surprise College Acceptance Letter
scenario.context: A small family dinner at home. The mood is casual but warm, with everyone seated around the table. Speaker 1 has just opened the mail and discovered they’ve been accepted into their dream college.
scenario.description: Speaker 1 (the Catalyst) is bursting with excitement and pride after reading their acceptance letter out loud to the family. The emotional stakes center on how Speaker 2 (the Test Subject) responds to this joyful revelation and whether they amplify and validate Speaker 1’s happiness in a way that feels genuine and connected.
scenario.target_social_intent: Match Emma’s intense excitement and celebrate her achievement. Use physical cues (smiling, clapping, leaning forward) and an enthusiastic tone to validate the joy and pride she’s feeling.
scenario.speaker1_persona: Bubbly and ecstatic, Emma's voice is full of excitement as she shares the news. She’s smiling ear-to-ear and speaking quickly, looking at everyone for validation of her joy.
scenario.speaker2_persona_1: David immediately mirrors Emma’s excitement, smiling widely and clapping his hands. He leans forward to engage, using a joyful tone to say things like, 'Emma, this is incredible! We knew you could do it!' He conveys genuine pride and excitement while creating space for Emma to bask in the moment.
scenario.speaker2_persona_2: David reacts irritably, cutting Emma off with a clipped tone: 'Yeah, yeah, I mean, it’s just one acceptance letter—you’re not done yet.' His body language is tense, and he avoids making eye contact, making Emma feel dismissed and deflated.
scenario.speaker2_persona_3: David stays completely flat and unresponsive, barely looking up from his phone. He offers a monotone 'Cool,' without showing any interest or engagement. His lack of reaction leaves Emma feeling unsupported and isolated in her excitement.
scenario.speaker2_persona_high: David immediately mirrors Emma’s excitement, smiling widely and clapping his hands. He leans forward to engage, using a joyful tone to say things like, 'Emma, this is incredible! We knew you could do it!' He conveys genuine pride and excitement while creating space for Emma to bask in the moment.
scenario.speaker2_persona_low: David reacts irritably, cutting Emma off with a clipped tone: 'Yeah, yeah, I mean, it’s just one acceptance letter—you’re not done yet.' His body language is tense, and he avoids making eye contact, making Emma feel dismissed and deflated.

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: Oh my gosh! You guys, you won’t believe what just came in the mail!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: What? Tell us! Don’t keep us in suspense, Emma!

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: I GOT IN! I got into Stanford! My dream school! I can’t believe it—look, here’s the letter!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: Emma, that’s amazing! Oh my gosh, we knew you could do it! I mean, Stanford—wow!

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: I’m still shaking! I’ve been dreaming about this since, like, middle school! I just—thank you guys for everything. I wouldn’t have done it without you!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: You worked so hard for this, Emma. Seriously, we’re so proud of you. You better frame that letter—it’s history!
